summarization.a: The XPS survey spectrum displays elemental peaks (Al, O, F, C) across a wide binding-energy range, indicating the chemical composition of the CF₄-etched Al₂O₃ film surface.
summarization.b: The high-resolution Al 2p spectrum shows peak deconvolution into components corresponding to Al–F, Al–O–F, and Al–O bonding states, revealing surface chemical modification after CF₄ plasma exposure.

d.question: How do the XPS results help evaluate the effectiveness of CF₄ plasma etching of Al₂O₃?
d.answer: The XPS spectra reveal the chemical changes induced by CF₄ plasma, showing strong fluorine incorporation and partial conversion of Al–O to Al–F and Al–OF species. These transformations confirm that the plasma successfully breaks Al–O bonds and forms volatile by-products during the etching process. The presence and relative intensities of fluorinated species give insight into etch progress, chemical reactivity, and the efficiency of surface modification under the stated pressure and power conditions.

b.question: At approximately what binding energy does the Al 2p peak appear in the narrow scan (panel b), and how does its intensity compare to adjacent chemical states?
b.answer: The Al 2p peak appears at ~74.5 eV with the highest intensity, while adjacent Al–OF and Al–F components show lower peak intensities.

a.question: Does the XPS survey spectrum in panel (a) indicate that CF₄ plasma treatment introduces fluorine onto the Al₂O₃ surface?

c.question: What chemical states of aluminum are identified in the Al 2p deconvolution?
c.answer: Al–O (main oxide peak), Al–OF (mixed oxyfluoride), Al–F (fluoride component)
